about.content:
The DDPD GenAI Project is exploring how artificial intelligence and automation can improve web content design for Canada.ca.

The AI Design Assistant (AIDA) helps content designers manage projects, analyze pages, identify issues, and create prototypes.

What it does now

  • Manage projects and content inventory
  • Visualize information architecture
  • Generate metadata with AI assistance
  • Export prototypes to GitHub for testing

What's coming next

  • AI-assisted content review
  • Accessibility checking
  • Automated quality assurance tools

We're building this tool step by step based on user feedback. Release 1 focuses on project management and prototyping. Future releases will add more content review and quality assurance features.

Interested in testing or contributing? Contact CDIA

dev.colors.description: Generate accessible colour gradients for AIDA
dev.colors.description2: . Use the colour pickers on this page to choose colours with a contrast ratio above 4.5 to meet AA WCAG requirements or above 7.0 for AAA compliance.
dev.description: Use these tools for designing and maintaining AIDA. If you're not familiar with these, you likely don't need them. Check with your team lead or return to your project.
dev.patterns.button.danger.desc: Use for destructive actions like deleting or removing content. Always pair with a secondary cancel button to prevent accidental deletions.
dev.patterns.button.icon.desc: Use when space is limited or for repeated actions in toolbars and lists. Always include a tooltip for accessibility.
dev.patterns.button.primary.desc: Use for the main positive action on a page or form. Pair with a secondary button for cancel actions.
dev.patterns.button.task.desc: Use for secondary navigation or auxiliary tasks that are supplementary to the main workflow, like accessing settings.
dev.patterns.card.desc: Use for grouping related content without interaction. The card itself is not clickable.
dev.patterns.cardHover.desc: Use when the entire card is clickable and leads to a specific action or page.
dev.patterns.description: Consistent design patterns for AIDA development
dev.patterns.input.select.desc: Use for choosing from a predefined list of options.
dev.patterns.input.select.label: Select an option
dev.patterns.input.text.desc: Use for single-line text input like names or URLs.
dev.patterns.input.text.label: Project name
dev.patterns.input.textarea.desc: Use for multi-line text input or when users need to enter multiple values.
dev.patterns.input.textarea.label: Enter Canada.ca URLs (one per line)
dev.patterns.message.error.desc: Use to notify users of errors or failures that require attention or action to resolve.
dev.patterns.message.info.desc: Use to notify users of of an optional action they should take. Some app features may not be available until the action is completed but most features are available.
dev.patterns.message.success.desc: Use to confirm successful completion of an action or operation.
dev.patterns.message.warn.desc: Use to alert users to potential issues or important considerations before proceeding.

github.connect.pat.description: Your token will not be stored beyond this session.
github.connect.pat.header: Sign in with your personal access token
github.connect.pat.token.help:
To push changes to a repository, you need a valid GitHub token. Your token works like a username and password, so keep it private and do not share it with anyone.

Follow these steps to create a new GitHub token:

  1. Go to your GitHub Settings.
  2. Select Developer settings.
  3. Click Personal Access Tokens > Fine-grained tokens.
  4. Click Generate new token.
  5. Give your token a unique name and a description.
  6. Under Resource owner, select either {{defaultOrg}} or your personal account.
  7. Under Repository access, select All repositories.
  8. Add the necessary repository permissions:
    • Administration (Access: Read and write)
    • Contents (Access: Read and write)
    • Metadata (Access: Read-only)
    • Pages (Access: Read and write)
  9. Add organization permissions:
    • Members (Access: Read-only)
  10. Copy the generated token and enter it into the GitHub token field whenever you push changes to GitHub.

Important: Keep this token secret. Treat it like a password.
github.connect.unverified: {{User}} can export to the selected repo but we cannot verify the scope of personal access tokens. If export fails, please manually verify your token scope.
github.connect.warning.details: You may not have write access to the selected repository.
github.export.description: The pages will be exported in Jekyll format. Redirects for the included pages are automatically set up, so you don't need to update links in your repo manually. If you create new pages directly in GitHub, remember to list them in your /source/data/exclude-redirect-links.json file.
github.export.error.other: Failed to set up repository. Please check your permissions and try again.
github.export.error.readOnlyToken: Your GitHub token doesn't have write permissions. Update your token permissions in GitHub and try again.
github.export.language.label: Export language
github.warning: Sign in with GitHub or enter your GitHub token to export your content

notFound.message.0:
404: Synergy Not Found

This page failed to align with our Q3 objectives and has been offboarded.
We appreciate your commitment to innovation by discovering this undocumented feature.

What now?

  • Regroup on homepage
  • Escalate to someone else's to-do list
  • Or just stare into the void for a bit. We won't judge.
notFound.message.1:
404: Page Deprecated

This resource has been sunset following a comprehensive stakeholder analysis.
Rest assured, we've pivoted to a more agile, cloud-native nothing.

Next steps:

  • Return to mission control
  • Touch base with your browser's back button
  • Take this offline and circle back never
notFound.message.2:
404: Critical Misalignment

This page failed to cascade properly through our information architecture.
A cross-functional tiger team is currently conducting a root cause analysis.

Available remediation paths:

  • Escalate to homepage
  • File a change request (processing time: 6-8 weeks)
  • Accept this as a known issue and move forward
notFound.message.3:
404: Above the Fold Anomaly

This page's wireframe exceeded our maximum clickthrough threshold.
After extensive user journey mapping, we determined it needed more alerts. Way more alerts.

Design system alternatives:

  • Realign with the dashboard of truth
  • Consult the style guide you never read
  • Just add more alerts. That always works.
notFound.message.4:
404: Existential Redirect

This page wandered off during a philosophical inquiry about its own existence.
Last seen questioning whether it even exists if no one clicks on it.

Coping mechanisms:

  • Return to the certainty of homepage
  • Contemplate the nature of broken links
  • Refresh repeatedly and hope for different results
notFound.message.5:
404: Bandwidth Reallocation Event

This page has been temporarily reassigned to support higher-priority initiatives.
Per the steering committee's unanimous consensus, all resources are now optimizing for maximum throughput.

Recommended action items:

  • Leverage homepage synergies
  • Schedule a standing meeting to discuss this later
  • Add it to the backlog and never look at it again
project.bookmarklet.description: Drag and drop this bookmarklet to your bookmarks bar. Click it to toggle between GitHub edit, GitHub preview, and the live Canada.ca page. This bookmarklet only works for this specific repository.

standalone.bookmarklet3.description: Use this bookmarklet to check for link problems on Canada.ca. It checks for 404's, hard-coded preview links, wrong language links, duplicate canadasite, empty links, and utm codes.
standalone.description: These tools help with specific tasks. They are not connected to your project data. Any content they generate will need to be saved or added to your project manually.
switch.description: Manage your saved projects. You can start a new project or load an existing one. Projects are saved locally in your browser if you aren't connected to GitHub.
